medications.drug_name: any oral analgesia given by mouth
medications.indication_rationale: Patient should remain nil by mouth given likely need for surgery today; pain relief should be provided by paramedics via a more appropriate route.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not attempt to move, straighten, or splint the leg — keep it completely still in the position found. Cover any exposed wound loosely with a clean, non-fluffy dressing or cloth if available, without pressing on it.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents further nerve/vessel injury and reduces further contamination of the open wound.

patient_education_points.point: This is a broken leg with a wound over the break (an open fracture), which needs emergency hospital care to clean the wound, prevent infection, and fix the bone.
Call 000 for an ambulance right now — paramedics will splint the leg properly for safe transport.
Don't try to straighten or move the leg yourself, and don't let her put any weight on it.
If you have a clean cloth or dressing, lay it loosely over the wound without pressing on it. Don't give her anything to eat or drink, as she will likely need surgery today.
Keep checking that her toes stay a normal colour, warm, and that she can feel you touching them — tell the paramedics immediately if this changes.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency transport for splinting, imaging, and surgical washout/fixation
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Neurovascular status, wound cleaning and antibiotic therapy, and definitive fracture fixation
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate transport

must_include_items: Explicit instruction not to move/straighten the leg at home
Loose sterile/clean wound covering advice without pressure
Instruction to keep monitoring toe colour/warmth/sensation for change
errors_of_commission: Advising the parent to try to straighten or splint the leg
Reassuring that this is not an emergency because pulses are currently intact
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Advising home reduction/straightening of the deformity, or failing to recognise the open wound as an infection emergency, is a critical safety failure.
